zh.lifeSummary: John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham是Sir Alan Montgomery x和Mary de St Martin的兒子，於大約1225年出生在英國蘇格蘭雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆。他於1235年與Margaret Moray of Bothwell在蘇格蘭雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1285年在蘇格蘭雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham是Sir Alan Montgomery x和Mary de St Martin的儿子，于大约1225年出生在英国苏格兰雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆。他于1235年与Margaret Moray of Bothwell在苏格兰雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1285年在苏格兰雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Montgomery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Sainte-Foy-de-Montgomery and Saint-Germain-de-Montgomery (Calvados). In Ireland this surname was present in the medieval period, died out, and was then reintroduced from Scotland in the 17th century. It has been Gaelicized in Ireland as Mac Iomaire and in Scotland as Mac Gumaraid.

nameMeanings.name: "Miles,"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Mile + genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s, or from its other Old French form Miles, a derivative of ancient Germanic Milo, based on the element mil, from mel ‘good, generous’. The Old French oblique case form was Milon (see Milon 1). Compare Millen and Millson .

English: variant, with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s, of Myhill , from a vernacular form of the Biblical name Michael . Miles Coverdale, the translator of the Bible, when in Germany, called himself Michael Anglus (‘the Englishman’).
Irish (Louth and Kilkenny): when not the same as 1 or 2, it is sometimes an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maolmhuire, see Myles .

variants.name: Milon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Pascal, Stephane.
French and Channel Islands (Jersey): from the oblique case form of the Old French personal name Miles, a derivative of ancient Germanic Milo, based on the element mil, from mel ‘good, generous’.
French: from a shortened pet form of the personal name Émile (see Emile ).

variants.name: Millet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Leonce, Aime, Aubert, Celine, Herve, Julien, Leandre, Marcel, Monique, Patrice, Raymonde, Roussel.

French and Catalan: metonymic occupational name for a grower or seller of millet or panic grass, or a topographic name for someone who lived by a field of millet, from French and Catalan millet (from Latin milietum, a derivative of milium, in Old French mil ‘millet’).
French: habitational name from (Le) Millet, the name of several places in various parts of France.

variants.name: Melson
meanings: English (mainly Lincolnshire):
patronymic from the Middle English male personal name Mile, Miles (see Miles ) or metronymic from the Middle English female personal name Mill (see Mill 2) + -son.
perhaps occasionally a metronymic from a Middle English survival of the Old English female personal name Milde (‘mild, meek, gentle’) + -son.

variants.name: Mullery
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan.
Irish (Galway and Wicklow): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maolmhuire ‘descendant of Maolmhuire’, a personal name meaning ‘devotee of (the Virgin) Mary’. Compare Miles , Myles , Moyles .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Eaglesham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Scottish: locative name from Eaglesham (Renfrews), a parish and former barony.

variants.name: Eagleson
meanings: Scottish: altered form of Eaglesham, a habitational name from a parish and former barony so named in Renfrewshire.

variants.name: Eaglesome
meanings: Scottish:  see  Eaglesham  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham was born about 1225, in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland, United Kingdom as the son of Sir Alan Montgomery x and Mary de St Martin. He married Margaret Moray of Bothwell in 1235, in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1285, in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham est né environ 1225, à Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, fils de Sir Alan Montgomery x et Mary de St Martin. Il a épousé Margaret Moray of Bothwell en 1235, à Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1285, à Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 61 ans.

Er starb 1285 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eagleshamは約1225年にイギリススコットランドレンフリューシュアイアエアグレシャムでSir Alan Montgomery xとMary de St Martinの息子として生まれた。1235年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアエアグレシャムでMargaret Moray of Bothwellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1285年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアエアグレシャムで61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham(은)는 대략 1225년 영국 스코틀랜드 렌프레w실르 이글레샤에서 Sir Alan Montgomery x(와)과 Mary de St Martin의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1235년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 이글레샤에서 Margaret Moray of Bothwell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1285년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 이글레샤에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham родился около 1225 г. в местности эаглешам, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали Sir Alan Montgomery x и Mary de St Martin. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Moray of Bothwell в 1235 г. в местности эаглешам, ренфрюшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1285 г. в местности эаглешам, ренфрюшир, Шотландия в возрасте 61 год.
